Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Continued To Dec-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kenneth Styles, via ZOOM, for Aaron Granquist, Petitioner; Brian Connors, via ZOOM, for Robert Spitler, Administrator; Andrew Zabronsky, via ZOOM, for Estate Research Associates, Petitioner; Janet Boessenecker, via ZOOM, for the San Francisco Public Guardian, Administrator of Estate of Elinor Dixon.

The court begins by asking Mr. Connors if he is aware that Resolve Bank in Ohio has failed and deposits over the FDIC limit have been lost. Mr. Connors reports that he was not aware of the preceding. Mr. Connors advises that funds have been reallocated to comply with FDIC limits and is not an issue now. The court then confirms with Mr. Connors that he is uncertain whether the funds are being held in financial institutions within this court's jurisdiction. The court instructs Mr. Connors to transfer funds to banks that operate only in California or into national banks that have branches in California. Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner Aaron R. Granquists First Amended Petition Pursuant To Probate Code Section 850 For Claims Of Adverse Possession And Equitable Apportionment
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom:
Brian Connors, Esq., appearing for Robert Spittler, moving party.
Kenneth Styles, Esq., appearing for Aaron Granquist, Petitioner in amended 850 petition.

The Court stated that this is a demurrer to the amended petition. Mr. Styles stated that he contested the tentative ruling as to part 1 of 2, that the principal issue is with the duty of the conservator, that in this matter there is no fiduciary relationship to the subsequent heirs and provided case background. The Court read aloud probate code section 2467, that the guardian or conservator continues to have duty of custody and conservation after death of ward or conservatee. Mr. Styles presented his oral argument in support of his position in terms of possession of the property. Mr. Connors responded with that he does not think there was a conflict between adverse possession and duty of conservator and that there was no final accounting ever filed, that Elinor took possession of property pursuant to distribution of estate, that Elinor is holding the property to the personal representative and that the Court's tentative ruling is correct. The Court and counsel had a discussion about the duties of the conservator and adverse possessor. Mr.Connors added that in addition to being a fiduciary Elinor was always and officer of the court, and as an officer she cannot be an adverse possessor.

Having heard oral argument and reviewing the court filed papers, the Court adopted the tentative ruling.

Summary:
Respondent's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. (See Evid. Code, sections 452, 453.) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.)
Respondent's Demurrer to the Petition Pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 for Claim of Adverse Possession and Equitable Apportionment is in part SUSTAINED without leave to amend, pursuant to CCP section 430.10(e), and in part OVERRULED.
The demurrer as to the First Cause of Action for adverse possession and relief pursuant to Probate Code section 850(a)(2(D) is SUSTAINED without leave to amend.
The demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for "equitable apportionment" under Probate Code section 850(a)(2)(D) in OVERRULED. Order for the subject to review.

Judge: Ross C. Moody, Court Staff Attorney: Elif Sonmez-Kirsch. Clerk: N. Wong, Court Reporter: Karen Audermaur. CSR #: 10919. Email: kaufdermaur@yahoo,com. Matter Reported.
